topic.what: First comprehensive 16S rRNA gene survey of the human gastric microbiome, revealing 128 phylotypes and substantial microbial diversity beyond H. pylori
topic.how: Culture-independent 16S rRNA gene sequencing from gastric biopsy specimens of 23 subjects, with phylogenetic analysis identifying bacterial communities across five major phyla
topic.why_matters: Overturned the assumption that the stomach is essentially sterile aside from H. pylori, establishing the gastric microbiome as a complex ecosystem relevant to gastric health and disease

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of distinct bacterial phylotypes identified in the human stomach
characteristics.vs_baseline: prior culture-based studies identified only a handful of gastric species
characteristics.context: 23 US subjects; diversity likely underestimated by clone library depth

good_for: methodological foundation for gastric microbiome studies

novelty: First culture-independent demonstration of a complex, diverse gastric microbiome
Identification of 128 phylotypes spanning Proteobacteria, Firmicutes, Actinobacteria, Bacteroidetes, and Fusobacteria in the stomach
open_problems: Functional roles of non-H. pylori gastric bacteria remain unknown
Whether gastric microbiome composition is resident or transient from oral/dietary sources
Impact of acid suppression therapy on gastric microbial diversity not assessed
